_comment: Adversarial JSON-mode flatten-append corpus (issue #44). Each entry in `bodies` is one request body fed verbatim (as raw bytes, whitespace included) to store.ProcessJSONAppend on BOTH the MemoryStore oracle and live Redis by TestJSONDifferentialCorpus. They pin the tricky framing cases the rapid generator covers stochastically: top-level array flatten-one-level, single non-array values, inner-vs-outer whitespace (which changes per-element byte length and therefore offsets), the frame-separator byte '|' inside string payloads, nested arrays/objects that must NOT be flattened past one level, deep nesting, and heterogeneous element types. `emptyArrayBodies` are the [] variants asserted under the create-allows / append-rejects asymmetry. These are committed regression fixtures: a divergence here can never silently regress.
